---
name: teams-blog-post
description: >
  Teams community posts — concise, casual, worth reading. No markdown syntax — plain text + emojis + bullets. 500-1500 chars. Hook → value → content → CTA. USE FOR: Teams post, blog post, announcement, tips, team update, community content, release notes.
---

# Teams Blog Post Skill

## Platform Constraints

### Works
- Plain text, line breaks, Unicode emojis ✅
- Bullets (- or •), numbered lists
- Max 10,000 chars

### Does NOT Work
- Markdown headers/bold/italic — shows literal chars
- Code blocks — shows backticks
- Links `[text](url)` — paste URLs bare

### Line Breaking
- One sentence per line (Teams auto-wraps)
- Blank line for paragraph separation

## Post Structure

1. **Hook** (1-2 lines): bold claim, relatable pain, announcement, question
2. **Value** (2-3 lines): what + why care; direct, no preamble
3. **Content**: tight bullets/lists; no paragraph > 3 lines
4. **CTA** (1-2 lines): question, invitation, link, next step

## Tone

### Do
- Talk like colleague
- First person ("we shipped", "I found")
- Get to point fast (30 sec attention)
- 1-3 emojis per post
- Vary sentence length

### Don't
- "I am pleased to announce..." — too formal
- Walls of text
- Buzzwords
- Bury the lead

## Length

| Type | Target |
|---|---|
| Quick tip | 300-600 chars |
| Announcement | 600-1000 chars |
| Achievement | 500-800 chars |
| Knowledge share | 800-1500 chars |

>1500 chars → cut.

## Checklist

- ✅ Hook first 1-2 lines
- ✅ No markdown syntax
- ✅ Within target length
- ✅ Casual tone
- ✅ Clear CTA
- ✅ No paragraph > 3 lines
- ✅ URLs bare
